name: Alaska Roll
description_short: A vibrant signature roll with a dynamic flavor profile.
description_long: This roll emphasizes bright, contrasting elements for a refreshing bite.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: Perfect for guests who enjoy a lively, multifaceted sushi experience.

name: Salmon Skin Roll
description_short: A rich and indulgent house specialty.
description_long: Carefully selected ingredients come together to offer a deep, satisfying taste.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: Pairs wonderfully with a robust sake or our house soy blend.

name: Volcano Roll
description_short: An intricate specialty roll crafted for a unique texture.
description_long: This creation focuses on the interplay of crisp and tender elements.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: A great choice if you appreciate textural variety in your meal.

name: Fire Cracker Roll
description_short: A bold, flavor-forward contemporary roll.
description_long: Modern sushi techniques are used here to create a distinct and memorable profile.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: Recommended for the adventurous diner looking for modern flair.

name: Hawaiian Roll
description_short: A delicate and thoughtful signature preparation.
description_long: Subtle flavors are layered carefully to maintain a clean, elegant finish.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with minimal soy sauce to let the natural flavors shine.

name: Green Dragon Roll
description_short: A hearty and complex specialty offering.
description_long: This roll combines substantial ingredients for a truly filling and savory dish.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: A satisfying centerpiece for any sushi combination.

description_short: A visually striking and flavorful modern roll.
description_long: Presentation and taste are equally prioritized in this engaging selection.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: A crowd-pleaser that works well for sharing.

description_long: Familiar foundations are elevated with modern touches in this specialty.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: An excellent bridge for guests transitioning from classic to specialty rolls.

description_long: This roll combines substantial ingredients for a truly filling and savory dish.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: A satisfying centerpiece for any sushi combination.

name: Phoenix Tail Roll
description_short: A visually striking and flavorful modern roll.
description_long: Presentation and taste are equally prioritized in this engaging selection.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: A crowd-pleaser that works well for sharing.

name: Black Dragon Roll
description_short: A balanced house roll merging classic and contemporary styles.
description_long: Familiar foundations are elevated with modern touches in this specialty.
cultural_note: Specialty rolls reflect the modern global evolution of traditional Japanese sushi techniques.
chef_note: An excellent bridge for guests transitioning from classic to specialty rolls.

name: Salmon Skin Salad
description_short: A crisp, savory salad highlighting toasted salmon skin.
description_long: Fresh mixed greens provide a bright contrast to the rich, smoky crunch of fried salmon skin. It tossed lightly to balance the savory and fresh elements perfectly.
cultural_note: Utilizing the skin of the fish is a traditional Japanese practice honoring the whole ingredient.
chef_note: An excellent light starter before moving onto richer sashimi or hibachi dishes.

name: Wasabi Shrimp
description_short: Crispy fried shrimp with a distinct wasabi kick.
description_long: Tender shrimp are lightly battered and fried, then accented with the sharp, clean heat of wasabi. The result is a savory, fiery bite with a satisfying crunch.
cultural_note: Wasabi is traditionally used in Japanese cuisine not just for its heat, but for its sharp flavor that cuts through rich ingredients.
chef_note: Great for sharing. The wasabi heat is present but manageable.

name: Fried Calamari
description_short: Golden, tender crisped squid rings.
description_long: Delicate calamari is coated in a light batter and flash-fried to ensure a tender interior and a crisp shell. A universally comforting and savory dish.
cultural_note: Fried seafood (agemono) is a beloved aspect of Japanese dining, emphasizing light, greaseless batters.
chef_note: A highly approachable dish that pairs well with our house dipping sauces.

name: Spicy Crawfish Salad
description_short: A bold salad featuring seasoned crawfish and crisp greens.
description_long: Succulent crawfish meat is tossed with a spicy, creamy dressing and served over fresh greens. It delivers a robust, savory heat with every bite.
cultural_note: While crawfish is non-traditional in classic Japanese cuisine, it has become a popular fusion ingredient in modern izakaya menus.
chef_note: Ideal for guests who crave a bit of spice early in their meal.

name: Jalapeño Yellowtail
description_short: Premium yellowtail sashimi accented with fresh jalapeño.
description_long: The buttery richness of raw yellowtail is perfectly offset by the bright, sharp heat of sliced jalapeño and a touch of citrus-soy ponzu. A delicate yet impactful dish.
cultural_note: Pairing rich fish like Hamachi with citrus (ponzu) and heat (jalapeño) is a modern classic popularized in the late 20th century.
chef_note: Eat this in one bite to experience the contrast of the rich fish and the sharp pepper.

name: Tako (Octopus)
description_short: Tender, mildly sweet slices of cooked octopus.
description_long: Expertly prepared to ensure a firm yet tender chew. This octopus delivers a clean, sweet oceanic flavor that highlights the quality of the seafood.
cultural_note: Tako must be massaged or gently simmered to tenderize the meat while maintaining its signature texture.
chef_note: Appreciate the distinct, firm texture—a hallmark of traditional Japanese seafood preparation.

name: Surf Clam
description_short: Distinctive red and white clam with a sweet, firm snap.
description_long: Hokkigai (surf clam) offers a beautiful visual presentation and a unique, slightly chewy texture. Its flavor is remarkably sweet and mild.
cultural_note: Hokkigai is treasured in sushi for its vibrant coloration and clean, oceanic sweetness.
chef_note: A great choice for diners looking to expand their sashimi palate beyond typical fish.

name: Amaebi (Sweet Shrimp w/ Head)
description_short: Delicate raw sweet shrimp, served with the crisp fried head.
description_long: This premium shrimp is prized for its soft, melting texture and inherent sweetness. The edible, lightly fried head provides a stunning savory contrast.
cultural_note: Serving the fried head alongside the raw tail is a revered tradition that ensures no part of this delicacy goes to waste.
chef_note: Eat the tail raw for sweetness, and enjoy the fried head for its intense, savory crunch.

name: Ikura (Salmon Roe)
description_short: Vibrant salmon roe with a rich, popping texture.
description_long: Large, orange pearls of salmon roe deliver a burst of savory, briny flavor. They offer a luxurious, oceanic taste that coats the palate beautifully.
cultural_note: Ikura is often marinated in soy sauce and sake, a technique that enhances its natural savory depth.
chef_note: Let the pearls burst naturally—avoid pressing them with your tongue too aggressively.

name: Tobiko (Flying Fish Roe)
description_short: Crisp, tiny flying fish roe with a mild, smoky flavor.
description_long: These vibrant little pearls provide an unmistakable crunch. They add a delicate salinity and a subtle smoky undertone to the dining experience.
cultural_note: Tobiko is naturally pale but is often cured to achieve its bright, jewel-like colors and nuanced flavors.
chef_note: Often used as a garnish, but equally enjoyable on its own to truly appreciate its unique crunch.

name: Hibachi Teriyaki Filet Mignon
description_short: Tender filet mignon seared with a savory teriyaki glaze.
description_long: Premium cuts of filet mignon are grilled on the high-heat hibachi, locking in moisture. The classic teriyaki finish adds a balanced sweet and savory depth.
cultural_note: The term "teriyaki" refers to a cooking method (yaki) involving a glaze of soy sauce, mirin, and sugar that gives a shine (teri).
chef_note: A highly satisfying entrée. Pairs perfectly with hibachi vegetables or fried rice.

name: Hibachi Teriyaki Scallop
description_short: Plump grilled scallops glazed in sweet soy.
description_long: Large scallops are carefully seared to achieve a caramelized exterior while remaining soft inside. The teriyaki glaze enhances their natural sweetness.
cultural_note: High-heat grilling (teppanyaki style) allows delicate seafood like scallops to cook rapidly without becoming rubbery.
chef_note: These cook quickly; savor the tender interior layered beneath the dark, savory glaze.

name: Miso Soup
description_short: Traditional warm soybean soup with tofu and seaweed.
description_long: A comforting, savory blend of miso paste dissolved in dashi broth. It features soft cubed tofu, delicate seaweed, and sliced scallions.
cultural_note: Miso soup is a fundamental component of a traditional Japanese meal, historically consumed daily for its nutritional benefits.
chef_note: A perfect, light start to warm the palate before enjoying cold sushi.

name: Seaweed Salad
description_short: Crisp, refreshing marinated seaweed.
description_long: This vibrant green salad offers a satisfying crunch and a light, savory-sweet sesame dressing. A revitalizing and healthy appetizer.
cultural_note: Wakame seaweed has been harvested in Japan for thousands of years and is prized for its distinct texture.
chef_note: Excellent palate cleanser between different types of rich sashimi.

name: Avocado Salad
description_short: Fresh mixed greens topped with creamy avocado.
description_long: A simple, elegant salad combining crisp mixed greens with rich, freshly sliced avocado. Paired with our house dressing for a bright finish.
cultural_note: While avocado is non-traditional, its buttery texture has made it beloved in modern Japanese-American cuisine.
chef_note: A great vegetarian option that still provides a satisfying, rich mouthfeel.

name: House Salad
description_short: Crisp greens served with our signature house dressing.
description_long: A fresh assortment of mixed greens, providing a light and crunchy start to your meal. Delicately tossed with a savory, tangy dressing.
cultural_note: Salads in Japanese cuisine typically emphasize freshness and lightly emulsified, soy-based or ginger dressings.
chef_note: A universally appealing way to begin your dining experience.

name: Squid Salad
description_short: Savory, tender marinated squid salad.
description_long: Carefully prepared squid mixed with subtle aromatics and dressing. It offers a firm but tender bite and a clean, oceanic flavor.
cultural_note: Marinated seafood salads highlight the Japanese commitment to precise texture and balanced marinades.
chef_note: For those who appreciate varied textures—the squid provides a wonderful, gentle chew.

name: Crab Salad
description_short: Creamy and sweet crab mixed with fresh greens.
description_long: Delicate crab meat is gently folded into a creamy dressing and served over crisp greens. It balances sweetness with a rich, savory profile.
cultural_note: A modern izakaya staple, often enjoyed as a refreshing counterpart to hot dishes.
chef_note: Rich and satisfying; pairs beautifully with a dry sake or light tea.

name: Edamame Bean
description_short: Warm, lightly salted young soybeans.
description_long: Freshly steamed edamame pods gently tossed in sea salt. A classic, nutritious, and subtly sweet appetizer that’s fun to eat.
cultural_note: Edamame literally translates to "stem beans" and has been a popular snack in Japan since the 13th century.
chef_note: Pop the beans out of the pod with your teeth. Excellent with a cold beer.

name: Spring Roll
description_short: Crispy fried vegetable spring rolls.
description_long: A golden, flaky wrapper encloses a savory mix of finely cut vegetables. These rolls are fried to a perfect crispness for a delightful crunch.
cultural_note: Inspired by traditional Asian rolling techniques, these provide a comforting, familiar appetizer.
chef_note: Best enjoyed immediately while they are hot and perfectly crispy.

name: Age Tofu
description_short: Lightly fried silken tofu with a savory dipping sauce.
description_long: Agedashi-style tofu offers a delicate, crispy exterior giving way to a warm, silken center. Served with a deeply savory dashi-based ponzu sauce.
cultural_note: Agedashi tofu is a classic Japanese dish dating back to the Edo period, showcasing the versatility of tofu.
chef_note: Let the tofu soak up a bit of the sauce before eating to fully appreciate the flavor.

name: Shrimp Shumai Dumplings
description_short: Tender steamed or fried shrimp dumplings.
description_long: Bite-sized dumplings filled with a sweet, savory shrimp mixture. They offer a comforting, soft texture with a delicate seafood profile.
cultural_note: Shumai originated in China but has been highly adapted into Japanese cuisine as a popular dim sum and bento item.
chef_note: A great starter for the table, offering a familiar and highly approachable flavor.

name: Gyoza Dumplings
description_short: Pan-seared savory meat and vegetable dumplings.
description_long: These classic potstickers are pan-fried for a crisp bottom, while remaining tender and juicy inside. Packed with a savory, umami-rich filling.
cultural_note: Gyoza are deeply ingrained in Japanese casual dining, evolved from Chinese jiaozi to feature a thinner wrapper and garlic-forward filling.
chef_note: Dip lightly into a mix of soy sauce and a drop of chili oil for an elevated bite.

name: Vegetable Tempura
description_short: An assortment of fresh vegetables in a light, crispy batter.
description_long: Seasonal vegetables are coated in an airy tempura batter and flash-fried. This technique locks in the vegetable's natural moisture and sweetness.
cultural_note: Tempura requires immense skill to achieve a batter that is lacy and crisp without being greasy.
chef_note: Dip gently into the warm tempura sauce, taking care not to soak the crisp batter.

name: Chicken Tempura
description_short: Tender strips of chicken in a light, crispy batter.
description_long: Juicy chicken breast is encased in our signature tempura batter and fried until golden. A savory, substantial, and comforting appetizer.
cultural_note: While seafood and vegetables are traditional, chicken tempura (toriten) is a popular regional specialty from Oita Prefecture.
chef_note: A fantastic choice for diners who prefer poultry or are new to Japanese cuisine.

name: Shrimp Tempura
description_short: Golden, crispy fried shrimp.
description_long: Large shrimp are stretched and coated in a delicate, crisp tempura batter. They remain sweet and tender inside their airy, golden shell.
cultural_note: Shrimp (ebi) is the undisputed star of traditional Japanese tempura preparation.
chef_note: Eat while hot to experience the ultimate contrast between the crisp exterior and tender shrimp.

name: Crab Rangoon
description_short: Crispy wontons filled with creamy crab and cheese.
description_long: A beloved fusion classic. Flaky, golden-fried wrappers encase a rich, warm filling of sweet crab and smooth cream cheese.
cultural_note: An iconic Japanese-American fusion appetizer that pairs rich dairy with delicate seafood.
chef_note: Careful, the center will be hot! A universally popular, indulgent start to the meal.

name: Takoyaki Ball
description_short: Savory, round octopus dumplings topped with sauce.
description_long: A classic street food: warm, soft batter balls filled with tender diced octopus. Topped with savory sauce, creamy mayo, and dancing bonito flakes.
cultural_note: Takoyaki is the quintessential street food of Osaka, beloved for its comforting warmth and rich umami.
chef_note: They are served piping hot—pierce them to let a little steam out before taking a bite.

name: Tuna Tataki
description_short: Lightly seared raw tuna with citrus ponzu over fresh greens.
description_long: Premium tuna is briefly seared on the outside while remaining completely raw inside. Sliced thin and served with a bright, tangy ponzu sauce.
cultural_note: The tataki method (pounded or seared) dates back to 19th-century Japan, utilized to impart a smoky flavor to fish.
chef_note: The perfect balance of a cooked, savory edge and a delicate, raw center.

name: Avocado
description_short: A premium avocado.
description_long: This avocado offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Saba (Mackerel)
description_short: A premium saba (mackerel).
description_long: This saba (mackerel) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Tamago (Egg)
description_short: A premium tamago (egg).
description_long: This tamago (egg) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Kani (Crab)
description_short: A premium kani (crab).
description_long: This kani (crab) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Inari (Tofu Skin)
description_short: A premium inari (tofu skin).
description_long: This inari (tofu skin) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Ebi (Shrimp)
description_short: A premium ebi (shrimp).
description_long: This ebi (shrimp) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Sake (Salmon)
description_short: Rich, buttery raw salmon.
description_long: Our salmon offers a luxurious, melt-in-your-mouth texture. Its high fat content provides a deeply savory, naturally sweet flavor.
cultural_note: Modern Atlantic and Pacific salmon have become standard sushi fare due to their reliable richness and vibrant color.
chef_note: The higher fat content means this fish pairs exceptionally well with a touch more wasabi.

name: Tai (Tilapia)
description_short: A premium tai (tilapia).
description_long: This tai (tilapia) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Tuna
description_short: Classic, lean raw tuna with a clean, deep flavor.
description_long: Akami (lean tuna) provides a firm, meaty texture and a profound umami profile. It is a fundamental benchmark of quality in any sushi offering.
cultural_note: Historically, lean tuna was marinated in soy sauce (zuke) to preserve it, a flavor profile highly prized in the Edo period.
chef_note: Savor the subtle metallic and deep savory notes without overwhelming it with soy sauce.

name: Escolar (White Tuna)
description_short: A premium escolar (white tuna).
description_long: This escolar (white tuna) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Tuna is a cornerstone of traditional sushi, prized for its varied, deeply savory profiles.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Smoked Salmon
description_short: A premium smoked salmon.
description_long: This smoked salmon offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Salmon became a mainstream sushi ingredient in the 1980s and is now globally favored for its rich profile.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Hamachi
description_short: A premium hamachi.
description_long: This hamachi offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Traditional nigiri emphasizes the natural flavor and texture of the seafood.
chef_note: Best enjoyed with a light touch of soy sauce to avoid overpowering the fish.

name: Unagi (Eel)
description_short: Warm, grilled freshwater eel with a sweet soy glaze.
description_long: Eel is expertly roasted to achieve a flaky, tender interior and a caramelized edge. It is finished with a rich, thick eel sauce that perfectly complements its savory notes.
cultural_note: Unagi holds a special place in Japanese culture, often associated with stamina and historically eaten during the hot days of summer.
chef_note: Since it is already heavily glazed, additional soy sauce is usually unnecessary.

name: California Roll
description_short: A mild, fresh roll with crab, avocado, and cucumber.
description_long: This ubiquitous classic perfectly balances the sweetness of crab with creamy avocado and crisp cucumber. A very accessible and refreshing choice.
cultural_note: Invented in the late 1960s in North America, the California Roll was instrumental in popularizing sushi globally.
chef_note: The ideal roll for a light lunch or for introducing someone to rolled sushi.

name: Spicy California Roll
description_short: A premium spicy california roll.
description_long: This spicy california roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Tuna Roll
description_short: A premium tuna roll.
description_long: This tuna roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Tuna Avocado Roll
description_short: A premium tuna avocado roll.
description_long: This tuna avocado roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Salmon Roll
description_short: A premium salmon roll.
description_long: This salmon roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Salmon Avocado Roll
description_short: A premium salmon avocado roll.
description_long: This salmon avocado roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Sweet Potato Roll
description_short: A premium sweet potato roll.
description_long: This sweet potato roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Spicy Salmon Roll
description_short: A premium spicy salmon roll.
description_long: This spicy salmon roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Spicy Tuna Roll
description_short: Fresh tuna mixed with a bold, spicy sauce.
description_long: Lean, clean tuna is elevated by a vibrant spicy blend, providing a rich heat that lingers pleasantly. A staple for those who appreciate robust flavors.
cultural_note: Spicy tuna is a brilliant adaptation that utilizes the rich texture of minced tuna combined with chili heat.
chef_note: If the heat builds up, a piece of pickled ginger will perfectly cleanse your palate.

name: Philadelphia Roll
description_short: Smoked salmon and cream cheese for a rich, creamy bite.
description_long: This roll pairs the distinct, savory smoke of the salmon with the dense, smooth texture of cream cheese. It offers a decadent, Western-inspired profile.
cultural_note: A testament to sushi’s adaptability, this roll merges traditional Japanese rolling techniques with beloved North American ingredients.
chef_note: Its richness makes it a very filling choice; consider pairing it with a lighter, vegetable-based roll.

name: Unagi Roll (Eel)
description_short: A premium unagi roll (eel).
description_long: This unagi roll (eel) offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Oshinko Pickles Roll
description_short: A premium oshinko pickles roll.
description_long: This oshinko pickles roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Cucumber Roll
description_short: A premium cucumber roll.
description_long: This cucumber roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Vegetable Roll
description_short: A premium vegetable roll.
description_long: This vegetable roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Shrimp Tempura Roll
description_short: Crispy fried shrimp wrapped in seasoned sushi rice.
description_long: The satisfying crunch of warm shrimp tempura contrasts beautifully with the cool, soft sushi rice. A dynamic and savory experience in every piece.
cultural_note: Incorporating hot, fried ingredients into maki (rolls) is a modern technique that showcases a wonderful contrast of temperatures.
chef_note: Enjoy this roll promptly while the tempura retains its maximum crispness.

description_long: This chicken tempura roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.

cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Crunch Roll

description_long: This crunch roll offers a balanced and fresh profile. It highlights quality ingredients carefully prepared. Ideal for guests looking for a savory experience.
cultural_note: Classic rolls (makizushi) represent the traditional, straightforward approach to rolled sushi.
chef_note: A very approachable option, great for rounding out a meal.

name: Yakisoba Vegetable
description_short: Stir-fried Japanese noodles with fresh vegetables.
description_long: Classic thin noodles tossed on a hot grill with crisp vegetables and a savory, slightly sweet yakisoba sauce. A hearty and comforting vegetarian dish.
cultural_note: Yakisoba is a staple at Japanese summer festivals (matsuri), revered for its savory, caramelized aromas.
chef_note: A robust and filling entrée. Add a dash of sriracha if you enjoy a little heat.

name: Yakisoba Steak
description_short: Stir-fried noodles featuring savory grilled steak.
description_long: Tender slices of grilled steak are stir-fried with traditional thin noodles and vegetables. Coated in a rich, savory-sweet yakisoba sauce.
cultural_note: Combining teppanyaki-style steak with street-style noodles creates a modern, hearty hybrid dish.
chef_note: The steak juices deeply flavor the noodles—a highly satisfying meal.

name: Yakisoba Chicken
description_short: Stir-fried noodles with tender chicken and vegetables.
description_long: Chicken breast is grilled and tossed with classic yakisoba noodles and fresh vegetables. A familiar, savory, and filling entrée.
cultural_note: A comforting, everyday dish in Japan, perfect for a balanced, hot meal.
chef_note: A great entry point for diners exploring Japanese noodle dishes.

name: Yakisoba Shrimp
description_short: Stir-fried noodles expertly tossed with plump shrimp.
description_long: Sweet, tender shrimp are stir-fried with thin noodles, vegetables, and our signature savory sauce. A wonderful seafood twist on the classic.
cultural_note: The high heat of the grill perfectly sears the shrimp while caramelizing the savory noodle sauce.
chef_note: For seafood lovers, this provides a lighter protein beautifully matched with the hearty noodles.

name: Hibachi/Teriyaki Steak
description_short: Classic grilled steak with a savory teriyaki glaze.
description_long: Quality steak seared on a high-heat grill to your liking, finished with our sweet and savory teriyaki glaze. Served alongside steamed rice and vegetables.
cultural_note: Teppanyaki cooking highlights the natural flavors of the meat, elevated by the caramelized soy-based glaze.
chef_note: A reliably delicious choice for meat-lovers seeking a classic Japanese-American dining experience.

name: Hibachi/Teriyaki Vegetable
description_short: A vibrant assortment of grilled vegetables with teriyaki glaze.
description_long: Fresh, seasonal vegetables are expertly grilled to maintain their crunch, then tossed in a rich teriyaki sauce. A colorful and healthy entrée.
cultural_note: Showcasing the natural sweetness of vegetables through precise high-heat grilling.
chef_note: A fantastic, filling vegetarian option that doesn’t compromise on savory depth.

name: Hibachi/Teriyaki Chicken
description_short: Juicy grilled chicken glazed in sweet and savory teriyaki.
description_long: Tender chicken breast is seared on the hibachi grill and generously coated in our signature teriyaki sauce. A universally beloved classic.
cultural_note: Chicken teriyaki is perhaps the most globally recognized Japanese dish, beloved for its comforting sweet-soy profile.
chef_note: Simple, satisfying, and perfect for diners of all ages.

name: Hibachi/Teriyaki Shrimp
description_short: Grilled plump shrimp with a caramelized teriyaki finish.
description_long: Large shrimp are quickly seared to remain sweet and tender, then glazed with savory teriyaki sauce. A wonderful, light, and flavorful entrée.
cultural_note: The rapid cooking of shrimp on a flat iron grill prevents them from becoming tough, ensuring perfect texture.
chef_note: Be sure to enjoy the shrimp alongside the provided vegetables to balance the rich glaze.

name: Katsu Chicken
description_short: Crispy, panko-breaded fried chicken cutlet.
description_long: A generous chicken breast coated in light, airy panko breadcrumbs and fried to a perfect golden brown. Served with a tangy, savory katsu sauce.
cultural_note: Yoshoku (Western-influenced Japanese cuisine) introduced breaded cutlets (katsu) in the late 19th century.
chef_note: The ultimate Japanese comfort food. The panko crunch provides a deeply satisfying texture.

name: Fried Rice
description_short: Classic savory fried rice tossed with egg and vegetables.
description_long: Steamed rice is wok-tossed at high heat with egg, finely chopped vegetables, and savory soy seasoning. A comforting, flavorful side or base.
cultural_note: Japanese-style fried rice (chahan) often uses short-grain rice, giving it a slightly chewier, pleasing texture.
chef_note: An essential upgrade to any hibachi or teriyaki entrée.

name: White Rice
description_short: Perfectly steamed, premium short-grain white rice.
description_long: Fluffy, slightly sticky Japanese-style short-grain rice. The perfect neutral base to absorb rich sauces and complement savory dishes.
cultural_note: Rice (gohan) is the absolute foundation of the Japanese diet and is treated with immense respect in its preparation.
chef_note: Essential for pairing with teriyaki or tempura to balance the meal.

name: Mochi Ice Cream
description_short: Sweet ice cream wrapped in a soft, chewy rice dough.
description_long: A delightful fusion dessert featuring premium ice cream encased in sweet, pounded sticky rice (mochi). It offers a unique textural contrast of soft, chewy, and melting.
cultural_note: Mochi has been enjoyed in Japan for over a millennium, traditionally pounded by hand during the new year.
chef_note: Eat these with your hands! Let them sit for a minute so the mochi softens slightly.

name: Soy Sauce
description_short: Our house soy sauce.
description_long: Enhance your meal with our specially prepared soy sauce. Perfect for dipping or drizzling to add an extra layer of flavor.
cultural_note: Condiments provide the essential finishing touches to customize the flavor balance of Japanese dishes.
chef_note: Use sparingly to ensure the natural flavors of the seafood and ingredients are not overpowered.

name: Low Sodium Soy Sauce
description_short: Our house low sodium soy sauce.
description_long: Enhance your meal with our specially prepared low sodium soy sauce. Perfect for dipping or drizzling to add an extra layer of flavor.
cultural_note: Condiments provide the essential finishing touches to customize the flavor balance of Japanese dishes.
chef_note: Use sparingly to ensure the natural flavors of the seafood and ingredients are not overpowered.
